records.title: Clear street encampments and expand treatment-oriented street-order responses
records.promise_text: A 2025 order advanced the administration's street-order agenda, but the balance between enforcement and treatment outcomes remained unresolved.
records.impacted_group: Black unhoused people and Black neighborhoods affected by homelessness policy, encampment enforcement, and access to treatment services
records.summary: A 2025 order advanced the administration's street-order agenda, but the balance between enforcement and treatment outcomes remained unresolved.
records.notes: Included because Black Americans are overrepresented in homelessness counts in many jurisdictions, making federal street-order and treatment policy materially relevant. Evidence is incomplete because implementation and measurable effects will depend on state and local follow-through.

promise_sources.source_title: Agenda47: Ending the Nightmare of the Homeless, Drug Addicts, and Dangerously Deranged

records.title: Expand domestic production of critical medicines
records.promise_text: A 2025 executive order advanced domestic-medicine production goals, but measurable supply and access outcomes remained early.
records.impacted_group: Black patients and Black communities affected by medicine shortages, chronic-disease treatment continuity, and pharmaceutical supply disruptions
records.summary: A 2025 executive order advanced domestic-medicine production goals, but measurable supply and access outcomes remained early.
records.notes: Included because pharmaceutical shortages and supply instability can affect continuity of care in conditions that disproportionately burden Black patients. Evidence is incomplete because the order primarily changed regulatory and production incentives rather than producing immediate patient-level outcomes.

promise_sources.source_title: Agenda47: Returning Production of Essential Medicines Back to America and Ending Pharmaceutical Shortages

records.title: Require proof of citizenship and paper-ballot election safeguards
records.promise_text: A March 2025 executive order advanced part of the administration's election-integrity agenda, but full implementation remained incomplete.
records.impacted_group: Black voters and Black-majority communities affected by voter registration, ballot access, and election administration rules
records.summary: A March 2025 executive order advanced part of the administration's election-integrity agenda, but full implementation remained incomplete.
records.notes: Included because federal election-administration changes can alter access, verification burdens, and ballot-processing rules in jurisdictions with large Black voter populations. Evidence is still incomplete because implementation depends on agency action and election-system adoption beyond the order itself.

records.title: End federal DEI and equity-based government programs
records.promise_text: The administration ordered the termination of federal DEI and equity structures, but implementation remained an active multi-agency process.
records.impacted_group: Black workers, Black students, and Black communities affected by targeted federal equity initiatives and program design
records.summary: The administration ordered the termination of federal DEI and equity structures, but implementation remained an active multi-agency process.
records.notes: Included because the order directly targeted federal programs and offices previously used to address documented disparities affecting Black communities. Evidence is incomplete because downstream program, grant, and enforcement effects require agency-level follow-through.

promise_sources.source_title: Ending Radical And Wasteful Government DEI Programs And Preferencing

records.title: Expand educational freedom and school-choice options for families
records.promise_text: The administration launched a federal school-choice initiative in early 2025, but the effects remained dependent on later state and grant implementation.
records.impacted_group: Black students and Black families affected by school access, public-school quality, transportation, and alternative education options
records.summary: The administration launched a federal school-choice initiative in early 2025, but the effects remained dependent on later state and grant implementation.
records.notes: Included because school-choice policy can affect access to schooling options, funding flows, and local education quality for Black families. Evidence is incomplete because the order relied on later agency guidance and state uptake rather than immediate nationwide change.

records.title: Prepare Americans for high-paying skilled trade jobs
records.promise_text: The administration launched a skilled-trades workforce initiative in 2025, but measurable labor-market effects remained early.
records.impacted_group: Black workers and Black jobseekers affected by access to apprenticeships, workforce programs, and higher-wage skilled trades
records.summary: The administration launched a skilled-trades workforce initiative in 2025, but measurable labor-market effects remained early.
records.notes: Included because apprenticeship and skilled-trade access can affect income mobility and labor-market opportunity for Black workers. Evidence is incomplete because the order primarily set federal direction and targets rather than documenting completed labor-market outcomes.

records.title: Promote excellence and innovation at HBCUs
records.promise_text: The administration reestablished a White House HBCU initiative in 2025, with broader institutional effects still dependent on implementation.
records.impacted_group: Black students, faculty, and institutions at Historically Black Colleges and Universities
records.summary: The administration reestablished a White House HBCU initiative in 2025, with broader institutional effects still dependent on implementation.
records.notes: Included because HBCUs directly serve Black students and are central to Black educational attainment, research capacity, and professional mobility. Evidence is incomplete because the order created an initiative and coordination structure but longer-term funding and student outcomes still require follow-through.

records.title: Reform higher-education accreditation around student outcomes
records.promise_text: The administration launched accreditation reform in 2025, but institutional and student-level effects remained in an early implementation stage.
records.impacted_group: Black college students and institutions serving large Black student populations, including HBCUs and other minority-serving institutions
records.summary: The administration launched accreditation reform in 2025, but institutional and student-level effects remained in an early implementation stage.
records.notes: Included because accreditation policy can affect federal-aid eligibility, institutional oversight, and compliance expectations at colleges enrolling large numbers of Black students. Evidence is incomplete because the order set a reform process but did not itself produce immediate campus-level outcomes.

records.title: Reinstate school discipline policies without equity-based disparate-impact framing
records.promise_text: The administration ordered a review and replacement process for federal school-discipline guidance, but school-level effects remained unmeasured.
records.impacted_group: Black students and Black families affected by suspension, expulsion, referral-to-law-enforcement, and school discipline policies
records.summary: The administration ordered a review and replacement process for federal school-discipline guidance, but school-level effects remained unmeasured.
records.notes: Included because federal school-discipline guidance is directly relevant to racial disparities in exclusionary discipline affecting Black students. Evidence is incomplete because the order initiated a policy process rather than producing immediate nationwide discipline outcomes.

records.title: Increase federal support for state and local law enforcement
records.promise_text: A 2025 executive order expanded federal support and protections for local law enforcement, but downstream local effects remained early.
records.impacted_group: Black communities affected by policing, public safety policy, arrest exposure, and federal oversight of local law enforcement
records.summary: A 2025 executive order expanded federal support and protections for local law enforcement, but downstream local effects remained early.
records.notes: Included because changes in law-enforcement funding, training, and oversight can materially affect Black communities through both public-safety outcomes and police-contact patterns. Evidence is incomplete because community-level effects depend on later implementation by DOJ and local agencies.
